q.en: What is the main language of Brazil, and why?
q.zh: 巴西的主要语言是什么？原因是什么？

options.en: Portuguese, because Brazil was a colony of Portugal for three hundred years.
options.zh: 葡萄牙语，因为巴西曾是葡萄牙殖民地长达三百年。

options.en: Spanish, because most South American countries speak it.
options.zh: 西班牙语，因为大多数南美国家都说西班牙语。

options.en: English, because it is the world's most common language.
options.zh: 英语，因为它是世界上最通用的语言。

options.en: French, because France explored the region first.
options.zh: 法语，因为法国最早探索了这片地区。

explain.en: The book states Brazil was a colony of Portugal for three hundred years, and the main language of the country is Portuguese.
explain.zh: 书中说巴西曾是葡萄牙殖民地三百年，该国的主要语言是葡萄牙语。

q.en: In what year did Brasília become the official capital of Brazil?
q.zh: 巴西利亚是在哪一年成为巴西正式首都的？

options.en: 1960, after a huge, wild area was cleared and highways and a lake were built.
options.zh: 1960年，在一片荒野被清理、公路与湖泊建成后。

options.en: 1822, when Brazil became independent.
1950, when the decision to build it was made.
1985, when the government became a democracy.

explain.en: The book says Brazilians decided in the 1950s to build a new capital near the middle of the country, and Brasília became the official capital in 1960.
explain.zh: 书中说巴西人在1950年代决定在中部建新都，巴西利亚于1960年成为正式首都。

q.en: Why is the Amazon rainforest called the "lungs of the planet"?
q.zh: 为什么亚马逊雨林被称为"地球的肺"？

options.en: Its plants produce about 20 percent of the oxygen for the entire world.
options.zh: 它的植物制造了全世界约20%的氧气。

options.en: It is the windiest place on Earth.
options.zh: 它是地球上风最大的地方。

options.en: It has the most people of any forest.
options.zh: 它拥有所有森林中最多的居民。

options.en: It is the driest region in South America.
options.zh: 它是南美洲最干旱的地区。

explain.en: The book describes the Amazon rainforest as the world's largest tropical rainforest, known as the "lungs of the planet" because its plants produce about 20 percent of the oxygen for the entire world.
explain.zh: 书中把亚马逊雨林描述为世界最大的热带雨林，因植物制造全球约20%的氧气而被称为"地球的肺"。

q.en: Which animal is native to the Pantanal and is the world's largest rodent?
q.zh: 哪种动物原产于潘塔纳尔，并且是世界上最大的啮齿动物？

options.en: The capybara.
The toucan.
The electric eel.
The red-bellied piranha.

explain.en: The book's land section notes that the capybara, native to the Pantanal, is the world's largest rodent.
explain.zh: 书中"地形"一节提到，原产于潘塔纳尔的水豚是世界上最大的啮齿动物。

q.en: How many times did Pelé lead the Brazilian team to victory in the World Cup?
q.zh: 贝利带领巴西队赢得过几次世界杯冠军？

options.en: Three times — in 1958, 1962, and 1970.
One time, in 1962.
Five times in a row.
Never; he only played in club games.
options.zh: 从未；他只参加俱乐部比赛。

explain.en: The book says Pelé is Brazil's most famous soccer player and led the Brazilian team to World Cup victory three times — in 1958, 1962, and 1970.
explain.zh: 书中说贝利是巴西最著名的足球运动员，曾三次带领巴西队赢得世界杯——1958、1962和1970年。

q.en: According to the book, with which South American countries does Brazil NOT share a border?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，巴西与哪两个南美国家不接壤？

options.en: Ecuador and Chile.
Peru and Bolivia.
Argentina and Uruguay.
Venezuela and Colombia.

explain.en: The book states Brazil shares a border with every other South American country except Ecuador and Chile.
explain.zh: 书中说巴西与除厄瓜多尔和智利之外的所有南美国家接壤。

q.en: How does the Pantanal help protect the environment, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，潘塔纳尔是如何保护环境的？

options.en: It acts as a giant water treatment plant by removing pollutants and protects areas downstream from flooding.
options.zh: 它像一座巨大的水处理厂，去除污染物，并在汛期保护下游地区免受洪水侵袭。

options.en: It produces most of Brazil's coffee.
options.zh: 它生产了巴西大部分的咖啡。

options.en: It is where Brazil holds its Carnaval parades.
options.zh: 它是巴西举办狂欢节游行的地方。

options.en: It is the place where Brasília was built.
options.zh: 它是巴西利亚的建都之地。

explain.en: The book explains the Pantanal protects downstream areas from flooding and acts as a giant water treatment plant by removing pollutants.
explain.zh: 书中解释潘塔纳尔在汛期保护下游免受洪水侵袭，并像巨大的水处理厂一样去除污染物。

q.en: In this book, what does the word 'gauchos' mean?
q.zh: 在这本书中，'gauchos'（高乔人）一词是什么意思？

options.en: Cowboys of the South American grassy plains.
A type of rainforest bird.
Portuguese rulers of Brazil.
A Brazilian dance of African origin.
options.zh: 一种源自非洲的巴西舞蹈。

explain.en: The glossary defines 'gauchos' as cowboys of the South American grassy plains.
explain.zh: 词汇表将 gauchos 定义为南美草原上的牛仔。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about Brazil's land, people, animals, history, and culture.
options.zh: 向读者介绍巴西的地形、人民、动物、历史与文化。

options.en: To convince readers to move to Brazil.
To tell a made-up adventure story set in the Amazon.
options.zh: 讲述一个发生在亚马逊的虚构冒险故事。

options.en: To teach readers how to speak Portuguese.

explain.en: The book is a country profile organized by topics such as land, people, history, celebrations, and animals, showing it is written to inform.
explain.zh: 全书按地形、人民、历史、节庆、动物等主题组织的国家概况，表明其写作目的是说明介绍。

q.en: In what year did Brazil break away from Portugal to become an independent country?
q.zh: 巴西是在哪一年脱离葡萄牙成为独立国家的？

options.en: 1822, ruled by Emperor Pedro I.
1500, when Portugal first claimed the area.
options.zh: 1500年，葡萄牙首次宣称该地区为其所有时。

options.en: 1888, when slavery ended.
1985, when the government became a democracy.

explain.en: The book's history section says that in 1822, Brazil broke away from Portugal to become an independent country ruled by Emperor Pedro I.
explain.zh: 书中历史一节说，1822年巴西脱离葡萄牙，成为由佩德罗一世统治的独立国家。

prompt.en: The book calls the Amazon rainforest the "lungs of the planet" but also warns that more than one fifth of it has been destroyed by deforestation. In your opinion, what is the most important step Brazil should take to protect this rainforest, and why?
prompt.zh: 书中称亚马逊雨林为"地球的肺"，但也警告说已有超过五分之一因森林砍伐而被毁。你认为巴西应优先采取哪一步来保护这片雨林？为什么？

en: Think about the book's statement that the rainforest produces about 20 percent of the world's oxygen and is home to the widest variety of life on Earth.
Consider the causes of deforestation mentioned (farming, ranching, land for people) and who is working to save these areas.
zh: 想想书中所述：雨林制造全球约20%的氧气，并是地球上物种最丰富的家园。
考虑书中提到的森林砍伐原因（农耕、牧场、人类用地）以及哪些组织正在努力保护这些地区。
